top_categories.workout_context: 8,408ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY Bogota's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
75 miles on paved Colombian mountain roads with sustained burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
112ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Base fitness for GFNY Bogota. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY Bogota. Train it until it feels boring.
GFNY Bogota's paved Colombian mountain roads with sustained forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 75 miles.
At GFNY Bogota, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 8,408ft of climbing on paved Colombian mountain roads with sustained does to your power file on race day.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved Colombian mountain roads with sustained. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
76–85% FTP for extended efforts. At GFNY Bogota, tempo is the floor you'll live on. Make it feel easy.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for GFNY Bogota’s 75 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: GFNY Bogota with 8,408ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. Altitude: 2,650m at the start, racing above 3,000m — oxygen availability is 73-75% of sea level; Temperature swings: cold starts (8-10C) in the salt mines, mild at valley floor, cold again at summit.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 75 miles of paved colombian mountain roads with sustained climbing at extreme altitude in Zipaquira, Cundinamarca, Colombia.
